audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim wilderness-border and Sihon narrative retelling, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Seir/Edom, Moab, and Ammon boundaries; wilderness provision; Rephaim traditions; Sihon diplomacy, hardening, conquest, and total-destruction language.

verses.restored: We turned and set out toward wilderness by way of Sea of Reeds, as YHWH spoke to me, and circled Mount Seir many days.
YHWH spoke to me, saying,
Long enough you have circled this mountain; turn yourselves northward.
Command the people, saying: You are passing through border of your brothers, sons of Esau dwelling in Seir, and they will fear you. Guard yourselves greatly.
Do not contend with them, for I will not give you from their land even tread of foot, because Mount Seir I gave to Esau as possession.
Food you shall buy from them with silver and eat, and water you shall buy from them with silver and drink.
For YHWH your Divine has blessed you in all work of your hand; he knows your walking through this great wilderness. These forty years YHWH your Divine has been with you; you have lacked nothing.
We passed by our brothers, sons of Esau dwelling in Seir, away from Arabah road, from Elath and Ezion-geber, and turned and passed by way of wilderness of Moab.
YHWH said to me, Do not harass Moab and do not contend with them in battle, for I will not give you from their land as possession; for to sons of Lot I gave Ar as possession.
The Emim formerly dwelt in it, a people great and many and tall as Anakim.
Rephaim they also were reckoned, like Anakim, but Moabites call them Emim.
In Seir Horites formerly dwelt, but sons of Esau dispossessed them and destroyed them from before them and dwelt in their place, as Israel did to land of its possession which YHWH gave them.
Now rise and cross wadi Zered. So we crossed wadi Zered.
Days in which we walked from Kadesh-barnea until we crossed wadi Zered were thirty-eight years, until all generation of men of war came to an end from midst of camp, as YHWH swore to them.
Also hand of YHWH was against them to destroy/confound them from midst of camp until they came to an end.
When all men of war had finished dying from midst of people,
YHWH spoke to me, saying,
Today you are crossing border of Moab at Ar,

and you will draw near opposite sons of Ammon. Do not harass them and do not contend with them, for I will not give you from land of sons of Ammon as possession; for to sons of Lot I gave it as possession.
It too was reckoned as land of Rephaim; Rephaim formerly dwelt in it, but Ammonites call them Zamzummim,
a people great and many and tall as Anakim; YHWH destroyed them from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place,
as he did for sons of Esau dwelling in Seir, when he destroyed Horites from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place until this day.
And Avvim dwelling in villages as far as Gaza—Caphtorim who came out from Caphtor destroyed them and dwelt in their place.
Rise, set out, and cross wadi Arnon. See, I have given into your hand Sihon king of Heshbon, Amorite, and his land. Begin, possess, and contend with him in battle.
This day I will begin to put dread and fear of you upon face of peoples under all heavens, who hear report of you and tremble and writhe because of you.
I sent messengers from wilderness of Kedemoth to Sihon king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying,
Let me pass through your land; by road, by road I will go, I will not turn right or left.
Food for silver you shall sell me and I will eat, and water for silver you shall give me and I will drink; only let me pass on foot,
as sons of Esau dwelling in Seir and Moabites dwelling in Ar did for me, until I cross Jordan into land YHWH our Divine is giving us.
But Sihon king of Heshbon was unwilling to let us pass through him, for YHWH your Divine hardened his spirit and made his heart stubborn, in order to give him into your hand, as this day.
YHWH said to me, See, I have begun to give Sihon and his land before you. Begin, possess, to possess his land.
Sihon came out to meet us, he and all his people, for battle at Jahaz.
YHWH our Divine gave him before us, and we struck him and his sons and all his people.
We captured all his cities at that time and devoted to destruction every city—men, women, and little ones; we left no survivor.
Only livestock we plundered for ourselves, and spoil of cities we captured.
From Aroer on edge of wadi Arnon and city in wadi, even to Gilead, there was no city too high for us; YHWH our Divine gave all before us.
Only to land of sons of Ammon you did not draw near, all side of wadi Jabbok and cities of hill country, and wherever YHWH our Divine commanded us.

translation_notes: Edom, Moab, and Ammon are given protected territorial boundaries. Israel is told not to seize their land and to purchase food and water.
The chapter preserves layered memories of earlier peoples and dispossessions; these are ancient ethnographic traditions, not moral endorsements of conquest.
The peaceful transit request to Sihon is part of Moses' retelling, while hardening language shifts responsibility toward Divine purpose; this tension requires theological audit.
Verse 34 explicitly includes men, women, and children in total-destruction warfare. It must not be softened or defended as modern ethics.
Protected borders around kin peoples sit in unresolved tension with destruction of Sihon's population.
safety_note: Never use Deuteronomy 2 to justify genocide, child killing, colonial conquest, terrorizing populations, or claims that Divine hardening removes human responsibility. Preserve its protected-border ethic while rejecting the total-destruction campaign as a model for any modern people.
